---
title: "Instructions for Glossa Psycholinguistics Submissions"
shorttitle: "Instructions for authors"
shortauthor: "Smith & Jones"

author:
  - name: "Alice Smith"
    affiliations:
      - name: "University of Nowhere"
    email: alice.smith@nowhere.edu
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
  - name: "Bob Jones"
    affiliations:
      - name: "Institute of Made-Up Science"
    email: bob.jones@madeup.edu

abstract: |
  This document contains instructions for preparing a submission to
  Glossa Psycholinguistics using the **glossapsycho** Quarto template.
  It also serves as a demonstration of the template's formatting: the
  document you are reading is itself typeset using the template. Replace
  the YAML fields above and the section content below with your own
  manuscript.

keywords: [template, reference, example]

bibliography: example.bib

masked: false   # set true for double-blind submission (hides authors, shows word count)

header-includes:
  - \usepackage{float}   # enables H placement specifier for floats
  - \usepackage{gb4e}   # for interlinear glosses; use linguex as alternative (not both)

format:
  glossapsycho-pdf:
    keep-tex: true
---

# Installation

The easiest way to install this template is directly from GitHub.
Run the following command from your project directory:

```{=latex}
\begin{verbatim}
quarto add znhoughton/quarto-templates/glossapsycho
\end{verbatim}
```

Alternatively, if you have a local clone of the repository, you can
install from the local path instead:

```{=latex}
\begin{verbatim}
quarto add "path/to/quarto-templates/glossapsycho"
\end{verbatim}
```

Either method copies the `_extensions/glossapsycho/` folder into your
project. Once installed, set the format in your document's YAML front
matter:

```{=latex}
\begin{verbatim}
format:
  glossapsycho-pdf: default
\end{verbatim}
```

Then render with:

```{=latex}
\begin{verbatim}
quarto render your-document.qmd
\end{verbatim}
```

The extension folder is self-contained and can be committed to your
repository so that collaborators do not need a separate installation
step. The `_extensions/` folder should not be listed in `.gitignore` if
you want the template to be available to everyone who clones the
repository. To update the template to the latest version at any time,
simply re-run the `quarto add` command from above.

# Text Formatting

This document is typeset using the `glossapsycho` template. The body
text demonstrates the default font and spacing defined by the bundled
`glossaPX2.cls` class file. Inline formatting options include **bold**,
*italic*, `inline code`, and [small caps]{.smallcaps}.

Paragraphs are separated by a blank line. A single line break within
a paragraph does not produce a new paragraph. Line spacing and page
geometry are fixed by the class file and should not be overridden. Do
not add custom geometry, font, or spacing commands to your document.

Section headings use `#` for top-level sections and `##` for
subsections. The template numbers sections automatically; do not add
manual numbering to heading text [@Smith2020].

# Tables

Use an R chunk with `knitr::kable()` and the `#| label:` and
`#| tbl-cap:` chunk options to produce tables.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-example
#| tbl-cap: "A simple table showing condition means."
#| echo: false
knitr::kable(
  data.frame(Condition = c("A", "B"), Mean = c(1.2, 2.4), SD = c(0.3, 0.5)),
  format = "latex", align = c("l", "r", "r"), booktabs = TRUE
)
```

Cross-reference with `@tbl-example`, which produces @tbl-example.
The `booktabs` and `tabularx` packages are loaded by the template and
available for more complex table formatting. Keep tables narrow enough
to fit within the text width; the single-column layout accommodates
wider tables than two-column formats.

# Figures

Use an R chunk with `#| label: fig-xxx` and `#| fig-cap:` chunk
options to produce figures with captions and cross-reference labels.

```{=latex}
\begin{figure}[ht]
  \centering
  \fbox{\rule{0pt}{3cm}\rule{0.8\linewidth}{0pt}}
  \caption{A simple figure placeholder.}
  \label{fig-example}
\end{figure}
```

Cross-reference with `\ref{fig-example}`.
Figure captions are set in small italic type by the template's caption
style. Figures are floating by default and will be placed at the top
or bottom of the nearest page. For publication-quality plots, prefer
vector formats (PDF or SVG) over raster formats (PNG, JPEG) to ensure
sharpness at any print resolution.

# Equations

Inline math: $y = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x + \varepsilon$.

Display equation:

$$
Y_{ij} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{ij} + u_i + \varepsilon_{ij}
$$ {#eq-model}

Cross-reference with `@eq-model`, which produces @eq-model.

Multi-line display equations:

$$
\mu_i = \alpha + \beta x_i, \quad y_i \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_i, \sigma^2)
$$

Use display equations for any expression that is important enough to
be referred to by number. Number only equations that are actually
cross-referenced in the text; unnumbered display equations use the
plain `$$...$$` syntax without a label.

The `amsmath` package is loaded by the template and provides
environments such as `align`, `gather`, and `cases` via raw LaTeX
blocks. Inline math uses standard `$...$` delimiters. Avoid mixing
LaTeX math commands with Pandoc markdown constructs in the same
expression.

# Interlinear Glosses

Glosses are typeset with `gb4e` via raw LaTeX blocks. Add
`\usepackage{gb4e}` to `header-includes` in your YAML to enable
glosses. Use `linguex` as an alternative — but not both, as they
conflict.

Two-line gloss (`\gll` ... `\glt`):

```{=latex}
\begin{exe}
  \ex
  \gll Das Kind schläft. \\
       the child sleeps \\
  \glt `The child sleeps.'
\end{exe}
```

Three-line gloss with morpheme breaks (`\glll`):

```{=latex}
\begin{exe}
  \ex
  \glll un-believ-able \\
       \textsc{neg}-believe-\textsc{adj} \\
       un-believ-able \\
  \glt `unbelievable'
\end{exe}
```

Cross-reference examples with `\Next`, `\NNext`, or the `\ref{}`
mechanism provided by the package.

# Masked Submission

Set `masked: true` in the YAML front matter to enable double-blind
mode. This suppresses all author and affiliation information and
replaces it with an automatic word count provided by the bundled Lua
filter. The `shorttitle`, `shortauthor`, and `orcid` fields are also
suppressed in masked mode.

Remember to remove or anonymise any self-identifying references in the
manuscript body before submitting. This includes references to your
own prior work that would reveal authorship, links to personal
repositories, and acknowledgements. Switch back to `masked: false`
for the final camera-ready version.

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
